parameters.content:
# WF-01 — Shopify Order Manager (Main)

## Purpose
Core ingestion and reporting pipeline for Shopify paid orders.
Polls incrementally every 30min → writes one row per line item to a
rotating weekly Google Sheets tab → delivers an AI-generated HTML email report.

## Architecture — Two Parallel Tracks

| Track | Entry point | Role |
|-------|-------------|------|
| A | Get actual sheet name from Redis (1st Branch) | Week rotation & sheet creation |
| B | Get sheet name for branch2 from Redis | Order fetch, parse, write, report |

Both tracks are triggered simultaneously by the Schedule Trigger (and Manual Trigger).
Track A must complete before Track B writes rows — sheet existence is guaranteed
by the parallel execution timing + Redis state.

## Outputs
1. Google Sheets row(s) — one per line item, active weekly tab
2. AI run report email — HTML, sent via Gmail after each successful run
3. Volume alerts — Gmail + Telegram if orders ≥ 50 or ≥ 100 in one run
4. 0-order alert — Gmail + Telegram if Shopify returns no new orders

## Error Handling
Global error workflow: `gvhDaCea1oROHUrb`
Gmail send nodes: `onError: continueErrorOutput` → Telegram fallback → StopAndError
All Shopify/Redis/Sheets API nodes: `retryOnFail` (3 attempts, 5s delay)

## Dependencies
| Service | Credential | Usage |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| Redis | Docker internal | sinceId cursor, sheet name state |
| Google Sheets | OAuth2 | ShopifyPaidOrders_Spreadsheet |
| Shopify | OAuth2 `n8n-acess1_oauth2` | Paid orders, any status |
| Gmail | OAuth2 | aliboussechill@gmail.com |
| OpenRouter | API key | Grok 4 Fast (primary) / Claude Haiku 4.5 (fallback) |

## Canvas Index
→ Track A detail       : sticky "Track A — Sheet Management"
→ Track B detail       : sticky "Track B — Order Pipeline"
→ AI report detail     : sticky "AI Report & Volume Alerts"
→ Redis keys reference : sticky "Redis State Management"
→ Manual ops           : sticky "Resets & Configuration"

Author: Ali Boussecsou — ali-n8n.com

## Redis — State Keys Reference

### shopify:sinceid
Monotonically increasing Shopify order ID used as pagination cursor.
Ensures incremental fetching — only orders after this ID are pulled.

  GET → "Get last sinceId"            drives the sinceId IF branch
  SET → "Set max sinceId (NoTTL)"     updated each successful run, no TTL
  DEL → "Reset sinceId"               clears cursor → next run = full fetch

⚠️ No TTL is intentional. Cursor survives restarts and redeploys.
⚠️ Shopify returns max 250 orders per call. If > 250 orders arrive between
   two runs, some will be missed. Volume alerts exist to surface this.
⚠️ Cursor stale or corrupted → run "Reset sinceId" from canvas (manual only).

---

### shopify:sheet:name
Active Google Sheets tab name (e.g. "WEEK16_2026").
Read by both tracks independently at the start of every run.
Updated automatically by Track A when the ISO week rolls over.

  GET (Track A) → "Get actual sheet name from Redis (1st Branch)"
  GET (Track B) → "Get sheet name for branch2 from Redis"
  SET           → "Set sheet name" — only on week_changed = true
  SET (manual)  → "Reset sheet_name" — see Resets sticky

## Track A — Sheet Week Management

Runs on every trigger in parallel with Track B.
Goal: ensure the correct weekly tab exists before Track B writes rows.

### Week format: WEEK{n}_{year}
Examples: WEEK16_2026 / WEEK52_2026 / WEEK1_2027
⚠️ Year follows ISO week year, NOT calendar year.
   Week 1 of 2027 can start in late December 2026 — this is correct.

### Execution flow
1. Read stored tab name from Redis (shopify:sheet:name)
2. Compute current ISO 8601 week — Thursday-anchored algorithm, UTC
   Outputs: computed_name, week_changed (boolean)
3. Is New Week?
   FALSE → nothing to do, existing tab is used
   TRUE  →
     1. Create new tab in Google Sheets
     2. Write 20-column headers (HTTP PUT → /values/{tab}!A1:T1)
     3. Format header row (HTTP batchUpdate):
          dark green bg / white bold text / frozen row / per-column widths
     4. Set new tab name in Redis

### Column schema A→T
inserted_at, order_id, order_number, created_at,
customer_name, customer_email, is_vip, shipping_country,
payment_method, currency, tags, line_item_id,
product_name, variant, sku, quantity,
unit_price, total_line_price, total_discount, is_custom_item

## Track B — Order Data Pipeline

### Entry
1. Read active sheet name from Redis (shopify:sheet:name)
2. Read sinceId cursor from Redis (shopify:sinceid)
3. Has sinceId?
   YES → incremental fetch (sinceId param, up to 250 paid orders)
   NO  → full fetch (first run or post-reset, up to 250 paid orders)

### Shopify Order Parser (Code node)
Normalizes raw Shopify payload to a typed schema.
Reference currency: shop_money (USD via price_set.shop_money).
display_currency tracks presentment currency for reference only.
VIP detection: customer.tags includes "VIP" (comma-split, case-sensitive).
Custom item flag: product_id === null.

### Has Orders? (IF node)
FALSE → 0-order alert email + Telegram → stop
TRUE  → SplitInBatches loop (batch size: 1)

⚠️ "Get many orders with sinceId" has alwaysOutputData: true.
   The workflow continues with an empty payload on 0 new orders.
   "Has Orders?" is the explicit guard for this path.

### SplitInBatches loop
  batching branch → Split Line Items & Sheet Row Parser → Append row → loop
  done branch     → (parallel)
                     • Get Max sinceId → Set max sinceId in Redis (no TTL)
                     • Aggregate Orders for Email → AI report chain

### Split Line Items & Sheet Row Parser (Code node)
One row per line item. Order metadata repeated on every row.
Timestamps converted to Europe/Paris before insert.
sheet_name injected into each row from Compute & Check Sheet Week output.

## AI Report & Volume Alerts

### Volume Alert routing
Triggered from the done branch of SplitInBatches, in parallel with the AI report.

  Count Orders → Is count_order >= 100?
    TRUE  → Send >100 Alert (Gmail + Telegram fallback)
    FALSE → Is count_order >= 50?
              TRUE  → Send >50 Alert (Gmail + Telegram fallback)
              FALSE → silent (no alert)

⚠️ Threshold constant in "Aggregate Orders for Email" code node = 50.
   If you change the IF thresholds here, update that constant too.

### AI Email Report
"Aggregate Orders for Email" computes per run:
  orders_count, total_revenue, total_discounts,
  vip_count, total_line_items, custom_items_count, volume_alert (nullable)

AI chain:
  Primary  → Grok 4 Fast (OpenRouter)
  Fallback → Claude Haiku 4.5 (needsFallback: true, auto-activates on failure)

Prompt uses a fixed HTML skeleton with hardcoded colors.
AI fills real data only — no invented content, no hallucinated metrics.
volume_alert string passed directly to LLM when threshold is breached.

Output → "Create subject" (Set node) → "Send run report" (Gmail)

### Gmail error path
Send run report: onError: continueErrorOutput
  branch 0 (success) → silent
  branch 1 (failure) → Fallback Telegram — Run Report → StopAndError

## ⚠️ Resets & Configuration — MANUAL USE ONLY

These nodes are NOT connected to the main flow.
Execute them individually from the canvas only when explicitly needed.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Reset sinceId
  Deletes shopify:sinceid from Redis.
  Effect: next run performs a full fetch (up to 250 paid orders).
  Use when: cursor is stale, corrupted, or a full re-sync is needed.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Reset sheet_name
  Sets shopify:sheet:name to current ISO week name.
  Value computed dynamically — always resolves to the correct WEEK{n}_{year}.
  Use when: Redis key is lost, corrupted, or after a manual rollback.

parameters.content:
## WF-01 — Meta

Version    : 1.0.0
Status     : stable — production
Last update: 2026-05
n8n version: 2.15.1

### Changelog
1.0.0 — Initial production release
  • sinceId polling architecture (webhooks rejected)
  • One row per line item data model
  • ISO 8601 week rotation with Redis state
  • AI report chain with fallback LLM
  • Volume alerts (≥50 / ≥100 orders per run)
  • Global error workflow + Telegram fallback on all Gmail nodes
